heading: Prerequisites
body:
- If the cluster runs with authentication, meet the following prerequisites:

  - For replica sets, grant the [backup](manual/reference/built-in-roles), [readAnyDatabase](manual/reference/built-in-roles),
    [clusterMonitor](manual/reference/built-in-roles) roles and the [BYPASS\_USER\_WRITE\_BLOCK](manual/reference/privilege-actions)
    action on the admin database to the user that
    will run the migration process.

    - [clusterMonitor](manual/reference/built-in-roles) role is not required only if you choose to disable
      embedded verification and the source cluster version is 6.0 or above.

  - For sharded clusters, in addition to the required [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)
    and [clusterMonitor](manual/reference/built-in-roles) roles, grant the [backup](manual/reference/built-in-roles),
    [readAnyDatabase](manual/reference/built-in-roles) roles and the [BYPASS\_USER\_WRITE\_BLOCK](manual/reference/privilege-actions)
    action on the admin database to the user
    that will run the migration process. Ensure the specified user exists
    on every shard and the config server replica set.
    The user must have permissions that allow the following actions:

    - Stop or start the sharded cluster balancer.

    - Read all databases and collections on the host.

    - Read the oplog on the host.

  - Ensure that this user is authenticated using
    [both SCRAM-SHA-1 and SCRAM-SHA-256](manual/core/security-scram).
    To learn more, see [Source Cluster Security](live-import-c2c-security).

> **Important:** 
>
> Source Cluster Readiness
>
> To help ensure a smooth data migration, your source cluster should
> meet all production cluster recommendations. Check the [Operations
> Checklist](manual/administration/production-checklist-operations) and
> [Production Notes](manual/administration/production-notes) before
> beginning the Live Migration process.

> **Note:** You can't migrate a cluster in a down state. The cluster running MongoDB
> must be operational for the live migration to Atlas to occur,
> as Atlas migrates one cluster member at a time, starting with
> secondary members and finalizing with the primary member.
> Ensure your cluster is running before attempting migration.
